session.user:
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.
I need to explore the existing test files and documentation related to the json_command and MCP patterns in this codebase:
1. Find and read all test files related to json commands, MCP, one-shot commands: search in `tests/` for files matching `*json*`, `*mcp*`, `*one_shot*` 2. Read documentation: `docs/learned/cli/json-command-decorator.md`, `docs/learned/cli/adding-json-to-commands.md`, `docs/learned/integrations/mcp-integration.md` 3. Read the CLI tripwires: `docs/learned/cli/tripwires.md` 4. Read the testing tripwires: `docs/learned/testing/tripwires.md` 5. Look for any existing `machine_command` or `machine_schema` files that might already exist
Provide the FULL contents of each file found.
session.assistant: I'll systematically explore the codebase to find test files, documentation, and existing patterns related to json commands, MCP, and one-shot commands. Let me start by checking the documentation index and then searching for relevant files.
session.tool_result:
1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Agent Documentation 5→ 6→Before starting work, scan the read-when conditions below. 7→If your current task matches, read the linked document **before writing code**. 8→ 9→## Categories 10→ 11→- [architecture/](architecture/) — Explore when working on core patterns (dry-run, gateways, subprocess, shell integration). Add docs here for cross-cutting technical patterns. 12→- [capabilities/](capabilities/) 13→- [changelog/](changelog/) 14→- [checklists/](checklists/) 15→- [ci/](ci/) 16→- [claude-code/](claude-code/) 17→- [cli/](cli/) — Explore when building CLI commands or output formatting. Add docs here for Click patterns and terminal UX. 18→- [commands/](commands/) — Explore when creating or optimizing slash commands. Add docs here for command authoring patterns. 19→- [config/](config/) 20→- [configuration/](configuration/) 21→- [documentation/](documentation/) — Explore when structuring or writing documentation. Add docs here for documentation methodology and best practices. 22→- [erk/](erk/) — Explore when working with erk-specific workflows (worktrees, PR sync, Graphite). Add docs here for erk user-facing features. 23→- [erk-dev/](erk-dev/) 24→- [gateway/](gateway/) 25→- [hooks/](hooks/) — Explore when creating or debugging hooks. Add docs here for hook development patterns. 26→- [integrations/](integrations/) 27→- [objectives/](objectives/) 28→- [planning/](planning/) — Explore when working with plans, .erk/impl-context/ folders, or agent delegation. Add docs here for planning workflow patterns. 29→- [pr-operations/](pr-operations/) 30→- [refactoring/](refactoring/)

session.assistant: Great! I found the test files and documentation. Now let me read the key documentation files and test files:
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: "@json_command Decorator" 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding @json_command decorator to a CLI command" 5→ - "creating structured JSON CLI output" 6→ - "understanding emit_json patterns" 7→ - "implementing output_types validation" 8→ - "working with AgentCliError error handling" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "applying @json_command below @click.command in the decorator stack" 11→ warning: "@json_command must be applied ABOVE @click.command. The correct order is @mcp_exposed > @json_command > @click.command." 12→ - action: "using json.dumps without indent=2 in a @json_command context" 13→ warning: "All json.dumps() calls in @json_command commands use indent=2 for pretty-printing." 14→ - action: "raising an exception in a @json_command without using AgentCliError" 15→ warning: "Use AgentCliError(message, error_type=...) to ensure errors serialize as JSON when --json is active." 16→ - action: "declaring output_types that don't match the return annotation" 17→ warning: "test_output_types_matches_return_annotation() validates output_types against return type hints. Mismatches fail CI." 18→--- 19→ 20→# @json_command Decorator 21→ 22→Universal CLI JSON infrastructure for agent-optimized command output. 23→ 24→**Source**: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/json_command.py` 25→ 26→## Decorator Parameters 27→ 28→```python 29→@json_command( 30→ exclude_json_input=frozenset({"repo_id"}),
... [94 lines omitted] ...
67→3. Raises `TypeError` if neither is available 69→## Error Handling 71→`AgentCliError` extends `click.ClickException` with an `error_type` parameter. See `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/agentclick/errors.py` for the current class definition. 73→Import: `from erk_shared.agentclick.errors import AgentCliError` 75→When `--json` is active, `AgentCliError` serializes as: 78→{ "success": false, "error_type": "invalid_plan", "message": "Plan not found" } 81→Three error categories: 83→- **Validation errors**: `error_type: "invalid_json_input"` (bad stdin JSON) 84→- **Command errors**: Custom `error_type` from `AgentCliError` 85→- **Passthrough**: `SystemExit` and non-Click exceptions pass through unchanged 102→- Mismatches fail the test with descriptive error messages

1→--- 2→title: "Adding --json to CLI Commands" 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding --json flag to a CLI command" 5→ - "exposing a command via MCP" 6→ - "creating result dataclasses for JSON output" 7→ - "stacking @mcp_exposed and @json_command decorators" 8→tripwires: 9→ - action: "placing @mcp_exposed below @json_command in decorator stack" 10→ warning: "@mcp_exposed must be ABOVE @json_command. Correct order: @mcp_exposed > @json_command > @click.command." 11→ - action: "creating a result dataclass without to_json_dict() method" 12→ warning: "Result dataclasses should implement to_json_dict() for custom serialization. Without it, emit_json_result() falls back to dataclasses.asdict() which may not handle complex types correctly." 13→--- 14→ 15→# Adding --json to CLI Commands 16→ 17→Step-by-step guide to adding structured JSON output to existing Click commands. 18→ 19→## Decorator Stacking Order 20→ 21→The decorators must be stacked in this exact order (outermost to innermost): 22→ 23→1. `@mcp_exposed(...)` — MCP exposure (optional, outermost) 24→2. `@json_command(...)` — JSON infrastructure 25→3. `@click.command(...)` — Click command 26→4. `@click.option(...)` — Options/arguments 27→5. `@click.pass_obj` — Context passing (innermost) 28→ 29→See `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/list_cmd.py` for a working example of this stack. 30→
... [87 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: Erk MCP Integration 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding new MCP tools to erk" 5→ - "configuring erk as an MCP server for Claude" 6→ - "understanding the erk-mcp package structure" 7→ - "debugging MCP tool calls from external clients" 8→--- 9→ 10→# Erk MCP Integration 11→ 12→Erk exposes its capabilities as an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server via the `erk-mcp` package. This allows external MCP clients (e.g., Claude Desktop, other AI tools) to invoke erk operations. 13→ 14→## Package Structure 15→ 16→<!-- Source: packages/erk-mcp/src/erk_mcp/server.py --> 17→ 18→``` 19→packages/erk-mcp/ 20→├── pyproject.toml # Package definition, depends on fastmcp>=2.0 21→└── src/erk_mcp/ 22→ ├── __init__.py 23→ ├── __main__.py # Entry point: parses --transport/--host/--port, calls create_mcp().run() 24→ └── server.py # Tool definitions and _run_erk() wrapper 25→``` 26→ 27→The package depends on [FastMCP](https://github.com/jlowin/fastmcp) (`fastmcp>=2.0`) and is a thin wrapper that delegates to the `erk` CLI. 28→ 29→## CLI Wrappers 30→
... [110 lines omitted] ...
35→- **Standard wrapper** — Runs `erk <args>`, raises `RuntimeError` on non-zero exit. Used by hand-written tools (`plan_list`, `plan_view`). 36→- **JSON wrapper** — Runs `erk <command> --json`, pipes params as JSON stdin. Does NOT raise on non-zero exit — `--json` guarantees structured error output flows through to the agent. Used by auto-discovered MCP tools. 62→Dispatches a task for fully autonomous execution via `erk one-shot --json`. Returns structured JSON indicating success or failure. On success, includes the created PR reference and CI run details. With `dry_run`: preview without executing.

1→--- 2→title: Testing Tripwires 3→read_when: 4→ - "working on testing code" 5→--- 6→ 7→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 8→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 9→<!-- Generated from testing/*.md frontmatter --> 10→ 11→# Testing Tripwires 12→ 13→Rules triggered by matching actions in code. 14→ 15→**Test only success cases for batch commands** → Read [Exec Script Batch Testing](exec-script-batch-testing.md) first. Cover all four categories: success, partial failure, validation errors, and JSON structure. Missing any category leaves a critical gap. 16→ 17→**Use stateful failure injection (\_should_fail_next flags) in fake gateways** → Read [Exec Script Batch Testing](exec-script-batch-testing.md) first. Use set-based constructor injection instead. Stateful flags are order-dependent and brittle. See the set-based pattern below. 18→ 19→**accessing FakeGit properties in tests** → Read [Erk Test Reference](testing.md) first. FakeGit has top-level properties (e.g., `git.staged_files`, `git.deleted_branches`, `git.added_worktrees`). Worktree operations delegate to an internal FakeWorktree sub-gateway. 20→ 21→**adding a force-include entry without registering in codex_portable_skills()** → Read [Artifact Distribution Sync Testing](artifact-distribution-sync.md) first. The skill will be distributed but not recognized by the runtime. Add it to codex_portable_skills() in src/erk/core/capabilities/codex_portable.py. 22→ 23→**adding a skill to codex_portable_skills() without a pyproject.toml force-include entry** → Read [Artifact Distribution Sync Testing](artifact-distribution-sync.md) first. The wheel won't contain the skill. Add a force-include entry in pyproject.toml [tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel.force-include]. test_codex_portable_skills_match_force_include will catch this. 24→ 25→**adding a test for a new pipeline step without creating a dedicated test file** → Read [Submit Pipeline Test Organization](submit-pipeline-tests.md) first. Each pipeline step gets its own test file in tests/unit/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline/. Follow the one-file-per-step convention. 26→ 27→**adding a tracking list without documenting the tuple field order** → Read [FakeLocalGitHub Mutation Tracking](fake-github-mutation-tracking.md) first. Every tracking list must have a property docstring specifying the tuple format (e.g., 'Returns list of (pr_number, label) tuples'). Without it, test authors guess field positions wrong. 28→ 29→**adding monkeypatch or @patch to a test** [pattern: `@patch|monkeypatch\.`] → Read [Monkeypatch Elimination Checklist](monkeypatch-elimination-checklist.md) first. Use gateway fakes instead. If no gateway exists for the operation, create one first. See gateway-abc-implementation.md. 30→
... [123 lines omitted] ...
33→**allowing `import X as Y` for internal/project imports because it's convenient** → Read [Import Alias vs Re-Export Detection](alias-verification-pattern.md) first. Erk prohibits gratuitous import aliases for internal/project imports. Exceptions: genuine name collisions and well-known library aliases (pandas as pd, numpy as np, dagster as dg, matplotlib.pyplot as plt). 35→**asking devrun agent to fix errors or make tests pass** → Read [Devrun Agent - Read-Only Design](devrun-agent.md) first. Devrun is READ-ONLY. It runs commands and reports results. The parent agent must handle all fixes. 59→**creating a fake gateway without constructor-injected error configuration** → Read [Gateway Fake Testing Exemplar](gateway-fake-testing-exemplar.md) first. Fakes must accept error variants at construction time (e.g., push_to_remote_error=PushError(...)) to enable failure injection in tests. 67→**creating or modifying a hook** → Read [Hook Testing Patterns](hook-testing.md) first. Hooks fail silently (exit 0, no output) — untested hooks are invisible failures. Read docs/learned/testing/hook-testing.md first. 69→**debugging 100+ unexpected test failures with no obvious cause** → Read [Environment Variable Isolation in Tests](environment-variable-isolation.md) first. Check ERK_PLAN_BACKEND first. Although the env var is now obsolete (get_plan_backend() was deleted in PR #7971), legacy code paths in context_for_test() may still read it. Use monkeypatch.delenv('ERK_PLAN_BACKEND', raising=False) or env_overrides={} in fixtures as a defensive measure until full cleanup in objective #7911. 77→**implementing interactive prompts with ctx.console.confirm()** → Read [Erk Test Reference](testing.md) first. Ensure FakeConsole in test fixture is configured with `confirm_responses` parameter. Array length must match prompt count exactly — too few causes IndexError, too many indicates a removed prompt. See tests/commands/submit/test_existing_branch_detection.py for examples. 95→**renaming a user-facing string in CLI output and updating related test assertions** → Read [CLI Testing Patterns](cli-testing.md) first. Test assertion lag: after renaming display strings (e.g., 'issue' → 'plan'), grep all test files for the old literal before committing. Tests using old string literals against stale snapshots will silently pass — the failure only surfaces in CI on a clean checkout. 105→**testing code that reads ERK_PLAN_BACKEND or other environment variables via CliRunner** → Read [CLI Testing Patterns](cli-testing.md) first. CliRunner env var isolation: ambient env vars from the developer shell leak into CliRunner by default and cause intermittent test failures. Use CliRunner(env={'ERK_PLAN_BACKEND': '...'}) to override, or CliRunner(env={}) to isolate completely. Never rely on ambient env being clean. Note: mix_stderr parameter is broken in Click 8.3.1 — do not use it. 115→**tracking mutations before checking error configuration in a fake method** → Read [Gateway Fake Testing Exemplar](gateway-fake-testing-exemplar.md) first. Decide deliberately: should this operation track even on failure? push_to_remote skips tracking on error (nothing happened), pull_rebase always tracks (the attempt matters). Match the real operation's semantics. 127→**using context_for_test() with wrong parameter name for issues** → Read [context_for_test() Dual Implementations](context-builder-signatures.md) first. erk-shared uses github_issues= parameter, src/erk uses issues= parameter. These are NOT interchangeable — using wrong name causes TypeError. 129→**using context_for_test() with wrong parameter name for issues** → Read [Erk Test Reference](testing.md) first. erk-shared uses github_issues= parameter, src/erk uses issues= parameter. These are NOT interchangeable — using wrong name causes TypeError. 139→**using monkeypatch.chdir() in exec script tests** [pattern: `monkeypatch\.chdir\(`] → Read [Exec Script Testing Patterns](exec-script-testing.md) first. Use obj=ErkContext.for_test(cwd=tmp_path) instead. monkeypatch.chdir() doesn't inject context, causing 'Context not initialized' errors. 141→**using output=, should_fail=, or transient_failures= parameters in FakePromptExecutor** → Read [FakePromptExecutor API Migration - Gateway to Core](fake-api-migration-pattern.md) first. These are the deleted gateway API. Use simulated\_\* parameters (tests/fakes/) or prompt_results/streaming_events (erk_shared/core/fakes.py). See migration table.

1→--- 2→title: Cli Tripwires 3→read_when: 4→ - "working on cli code" 5→--- 6→ 7→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 8→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 9→<!-- Generated from cli/*.md frontmatter --> 10→ 11→# Cli Tripwires 12→ 13→Rules triggered by matching actions in code. 14→ 15→**Design batch commands that process items despite validation failures** → Read [Batch Exec Commands](batch-exec-commands.md) first. Validate ALL items upfront before processing ANY items. Stop on first validation error. 16→ 17→**Return non-zero exit codes for batch command failures** → Read [Batch Exec Commands](batch-exec-commands.md) first. Always exit 0, encode errors in JSON output with per-item success fields. 18→ 19→**Use OR semantics for batch success (success=true if any item succeeds)** → Read [Batch Exec Commands](batch-exec-commands.md) first. Use AND semantics: top-level success=true only if ALL items succeed. 20→ 21→**Using RuntimeError for expected CLI failures** → Read [CLI Error Handling Anti-Patterns](error-handling-antipatterns.md) first. RuntimeError signals a programmer error (bug in the code), NOT expected user-facing failures. Use UserFacingCliError for expected conditions (missing files, invalid input, precondition violations) that should exit cleanly with an actionable message. 22→ 23→**WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP maps command names to .yml filenames** → Read [Workflow Commands](workflow-commands.md) first. command names intentionally diverge from filenames (e.g., pr-rebase → pr-rebase.yml, but plan-implement → plan-implement.yml via DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME constant) 24→ 25→**adding --from-current-branch without handling detached HEAD** → Read [Slot Assign --from-current-branch](slot-assign-from-current-branch.md) first. When the target branch is checked out in another worktree, fall back to detached HEAD checkout. See slot-assign-from-current-branch.md. 26→ 27→**adding --json flag to a command without using @json_output decorator** → Read [Agent-Friendly CLI Design Principles](agent-friendly-cli.md) first. Use the shared @json_output decorator from src/erk/cli/json_output.py. Do not manually implement JSON serialization in individual commands. 28→ 29→**adding --sync to checkout** → Read [Checkout/Teleport Command Split](checkout-teleport-split.md) first. checkout is local-only; use teleport for sync. Checkout preserves local state; teleport force-resets to remote. 30→
... [199 lines omitted] ...
39→**adding a new step to the submit pipeline** → Read [PR Submit Pipeline Architecture](pr-submit-pipeline.md) first. Each step must return SubmitState | SubmitError. Use dataclasses.replace() for state updates. Add the step to \_submit_pipeline() tuple. 41→**adding a parameter to erk exec without updating calling command** → Read [Parameter Addition Checklist](parameter-addition-checklist.md) first. 5-step verification required. Parameter additions must thread through skill argument-hint, command invocations, AND exec script. Miss any layer and you get silent failures or discovery problems. See parameter-addition-checklist.md. 75→**calling is_learned_docs_available() in CLI code** → Read [erk docs check Command](erk-docs-check.md) first. Function signature requires repo_ops and cwd kwargs: is_learned_docs_available(repo_ops=..., cwd=...). Omitting either kwarg will cause a TypeError. 77→**choosing between Ensure and EnsureIdeal** → Read [EnsureIdeal Pattern for Type Narrowing](ensure-ideal-pattern.md) first. Ensure is for invariant checks (preconditions). EnsureIdeal is for type narrowing (handling operations that can return non-ideal states). If the value comes from an operation that returns T | ErrorType, use EnsureIdeal. 105→**flagging 5+ parameter violations in code review** → Read [Code Review Filtering](code-review-filtering.md) first. Before flagging, verify NO exception applies (ABC/Protocol/Click) 127→**making a separate subprocess call for each item in a batch operation** → Read [Exec Script Performance Patterns](exec-script-performance.md) first. Use batch exec commands pattern (batch-exec-commands.md) to process items in a single invocation with per-item error handling. 129→**making session discovery failures block the land command** → Read [Land-Learn Integration](land-learn-integration.md) first. Session discovery uses fire-and-forget error resilience. The entire learn workflow is wrapped in try/except to prevent blocking land. Failures are reported as warnings, never errors. 149→**plan-implement exists in WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP but erk launch plan-implement always raises UsageError** → Read [Workflow Commands](workflow-commands.md) first. use erk pr submit instead 155→**raising an exception in a @json_command without using AgentCliError** → Read [@json_command Decorator](json-command-decorator.md) first. Use AgentCliError(message, error_type=...) to ensure errors serialize as JSON when --json is active. 175→**returning non-zero exit code when --json flag is active** → Read [Agent-Friendly CLI Design Principles](agent-friendly-cli.md) first. JSON mode must always exit 0. Errors are communicated via {success: false, error_type, message} in stdout JSON, not via exit codes. 185→**testing prompts without matching confirm_responses array length** → Read [Prompt Consolidation Pattern](prompt-consolidation-pattern.md) first. confirm_responses array length must match the number of prompts. Too few causes IndexError; too many indicates a prompt was removed without updating tests. 187→**using EnsureIdeal for discriminated union narrowing** → Read [EnsureIdeal Pattern for Type Narrowing](ensure-ideal-pattern.md) first. Only use when the error type implements NonIdealState protocol OR provides a message field. For custom error types without standard fields, add a specific EnsureIdeal method. 199→**using ctx.invoke() with kwargs that don't match target function parameter names** → Read [PR Submit Pipeline Architecture](pr-submit-pipeline.md) first. Click ctx.invoke() forwards kwargs directly — any name mismatch causes runtime TypeError. Verify all parameter names exactly match the target function signature. 201→**using dict .get() to access fields from exec script JSON output without a TypedDict schema** → Read [Exec Script Schema Patterns](exec-script-schema-patterns.md) first. Silent filtering failures occur when field names are mistyped. Define TypedDict in erk_shared and use cast() in consumers. 223→**writing multi-line error messages in Ensure method calls** → Read [CLI Output Styling Guide](output-styling.md) first. Use implicit string concatenation with 
 at end of first string. Line 1 is the primary error, line 2+ is remediation context. Do NOT use 

 (double newline) — Ensure handles spacing.

1→"""Tests for @json_command that depend on the erk CLI tree.""" 2→ 3→import types 4→import typing 5→ 6→import click 7→ 8→from erk.cli.cli import cli 9→ 10→# -- output_types validation test -- 11→ 12→ 13→def test_output_types_matches_return_annotation() -> None: 14→ """Every @json_command's output_types must match its return annotation.""" 15→ 16→ def _collect_json_commands(group: click.BaseCommand) -> list[click.BaseCommand]: 17→ """Recursively collect all commands with _json_command_meta.""" 18→ result = [] 19→ if hasattr(group, "_json_command_meta"): 20→ result.append(group) 21→ if isinstance(group, click.Group): 22→ for cmd in group.commands.values(): 23→ result.extend(_collect_json_commands(cmd)) 24→ return result 25→ 26→ def _unwrap_return_types(annotation: typing.Any) -> set[type]: 27→ """Decompose A | B unions into member types.""" 28→ origin = typing.get_origin(annotation) 29→ if origin is typing.Union or isinstance(annotation, types.UnionType): 30→ return set(typing.get_args(annotation))

1→"""Parity tests: every @mcp_exposed command is discovered and has correct schema.""" 2→ 3→from __future__ import annotations 4→ 5→import asyncio 6→ 7→from erk.cli.cli import cli 8→from erk_mcp.server import _build_json_command_tools, create_mcp 9→from erk_shared.agentclick.json_schema import command_input_schema 10→from erk_shared.agentclick.mcp_exposed import discover_mcp_commands 11→ 12→ 13→def test_every_mcp_exposed_command_is_registered_as_mcp_tool() -> None: 14→ """Every command with _mcp_meta in the Click tree appears as an MCP tool.""" 15→ discovered = discover_mcp_commands(cli, _parent_path=()) 16→ assert len(discovered) > 0, "No @mcp_exposed commands found — check CLI imports" 17→ 18→ server = create_mcp() 19→ tools = asyncio.run(server.list_tools()) 20→ tool_names = {t.name for t in tools} 21→ 22→ for _cmd, meta, _path in discovered: 23→ assert meta.name in tool_names, f"@mcp_exposed '{meta.name}' not registered as MCP tool" 24→ 25→ 26→def test_mcp_tool_schema_matches_click_derived_schema() -> None: 27→ """Each MCP tool's schema matches command_input_schema(cmd).""" 28→ discovered = discover_mcp_commands(cli, _parent_path=()) 29→ 30→ built_tools = _build_json_command_tools()

session.tool_result:
1→"""JSON command harness for CLI commands (input + output + schema). 2→ 3→Adds --json and --schema flags to Click commands with: 4→- JSON error serialization (catches click.ClickException with error_type attribute) 5→- JSON input from stdin (when piped, maps keys to Click params) 6→- emit_json_result() for structured output via to_json_dict() protocol 7→- --schema flag to output JSON Schema for input/output/error shapes 8→ 9→Commands return a result object with to_json_dict() or as a dataclass; 10→the decorator auto-serializes it via emit_json_result() when --json is active. 11→Commands may also call emit_json()/emit_json_result() inline and return None. 12→""" 13→ 14→import dataclasses 15→import json 16→import sys 17→from dataclasses import dataclass 18→from typing import Any, overload 19→ 20→import click 21→ 22→ 23→@dataclass(frozen=True) 24→class JsonCommandMeta: 25→ """Metadata stored on Command objects decorated with @json_command.""" 26→ 27→ exclude_json_input: frozenset[str] 28→ required_json_input: frozenset[str] 29→ output_types: tuple[type, ...] 30→
... [233 lines omitted] ...
52→ """Add --json/--schema flags, JSON input mapping, and JSON error handling to a Click command. 64→ - Any click.ClickException with an error_type attribute is caught and serialized as JSON 65→ - SystemExit and other exceptions pass through unchanged 68→ - Outputs JSON Schema document for the command's input/output/error shapes 96→ json.JSONDecodeError: If stdin contains invalid JSON 97→ ValueError: If stdin JSON is not an object 106→ raise ValueError("JSON input must be an object") 164→ except json.JSONDecodeError as exc: 165→ error_data = { 167→ "error_type": "invalid_json_input", 170→ click.echo(json.dumps(error_data, indent=2)) 172→ except ValueError as exc: 173→ error_data = { 175→ "error_type": "invalid_json_input", 178→ click.echo(json.dumps(error_data, indent=2)) 186→ error_data = { 188→ "error_type": "invalid_json_input", 191→ click.echo(json.dumps(error_data, indent=2)) 202→ error_data = { 204→ "error_type": "invalid_json_input", 207→ click.echo(json.dumps(error_data, indent=2)) 215→ except click.ClickException as exc: 216→ if not hasattr(exc, "error_type"): 218→ error_data = { 220→ "error_type": exc.error_type, 223→ click.echo(json.dumps(error_data, indent=2)) 248→ TypeError: If result has no to_json_dict() and is not a dataclass 256→ raise TypeError(f"Cannot serialize {type_name}: no to_json_dict() and not a dataclass")

1→"""Decorator and discovery for auto-exposing @json_command CLIs as MCP tools.""" 2→ 3→from collections.abc import Callable 4→from dataclasses import dataclass 5→ 6→import click 7→ 8→ 9→@dataclass(frozen=True) 10→class McpMeta: 11→ """MCP metadata attached to a Click command by @mcp_exposed.""" 12→ 13→ name: str 14→ description: str 15→ 16→ 17→_MCP_REGISTRY: dict[click.Command, McpMeta] = {} 18→ 19→ 20→def mcp_exposed(*, name: str, description: str) -> Callable[[click.Command], click.Command]: 21→ """Mark a @json_command for automatic MCP exposure. 22→ 23→ Apply above @json_command in the decorator stack: 24→ 25→ @mcp_exposed(name="one_shot", description="...") 26→ @json_command(...) 27→ @click.command("one-shot") 28→ 29→ Args: 30→ name: MCP tool name
... [41 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Command to dispatch a task for fully autonomous remote execution. 2→ 3→No local planning — the remote Claude explores the codebase, plans the change, 4→implements it, and creates a PR. 5→ 6→Usage: 7→ erk one-shot "fix the import in config.py" 8→ erk one-shot --file prompt.md 9→ erk one-shot "add type hints to utils.py" --model opus 10→ erk one-shot "fix the typo in README.md" --dry-run 11→ erk one-shot "rename issue_number to plan_number in impl_init.py" --plan-only 12→ erk one-shot "fix config bug" --repo owner/repo 13→""" 14→ 15→from pathlib import Path 16→ 17→import click 18→ 19→from erk.cli.commands.implement_shared import normalize_model_name 20→from erk.cli.commands.one_shot_remote_dispatch import ( 21→ OneShotDispatchParams, 22→ OneShotDispatchResult, 23→ OneShotDryRunResult, 24→ dispatch_one_shot_remote, 25→) 26→from erk.cli.commands.ref_resolution import resolve_dispatch_ref 27→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure, UserFacingCliError 28→from erk.core.context import ErkContext, NoRepoSentinel 29→from erk_shared.agentclick.json_command import json_command 30→from erk_shared.agentclick.mcp_exposed import mcp_exposed
... [208 lines omitted] ...
49→ UserFacingCliError: If no http_client is available 55→ raise UserFacingCliError( 57→ error_type="auth_required", 69→ "On error: error_type and message." 179→ raise UserFacingCliError("Prompt must not be empty", error_type="invalid_input") 196→ raise UserFacingCliError( 199→ error_type="invalid_repo", 204→ raise UserFacingCliError( 207→ error_type="cli_error", 213→ raise click.UsageError("--repo and --ref-current are mutually exclusive")

1→"""Dispatch logic for one-shot execution via RemoteGitHub REST API. 2→ 3→Uses RemoteGitHub gateway to create branches, commit files, create PRs, 4→and dispatch workflows entirely via the GitHub REST API. session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: One-Shot Workflow 3→last_audited: "2026-02-24 00:00 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "working with erk one-shot dispatch" 7→ - "understanding how plans are autonomously created and implemented" 8→ - "debugging one-shot workflow failures" 9→ - "integrating objectives with one-shot dispatch" 10→tripwires: 11→ - action: "adding dry-run support to one-shot commands" 12→ warning: "One-shot dry-run mode must NOT create skeleton issues" 13→ - action: "modifying register-one-shot-plan exit behavior" 14→ warning: "register-one-shot-plan uses best-effort: exit 0 if any operation succeeds" 15→ - action: "adding post-dispatch operations without matching dispatch_cmd.py pattern" 16→ warning: "dispatch_one_shot() and _dispatch_planned_pr_plan() in dispatch_cmd.py must stay synchronized. Both use write_dispatch_metadata() + create_submission_queued_block(). Changes to one must be mirrored in the other." 17→ - action: "writing post-dispatch operations without try/except guards" 18→ warning: "Post-dispatch operations (metadata write, queued comment) are best-effort. Wrap in try/except with user-visible warnings. See write_dispatch_metadata() and create_submission_queued_block() in one_shot_dispatch.py." 19→ - action: "editing plan body content in plan creation, replan, or one-shot dispatch" 20→ warning: "One-shot metadata block preservation: the metadata block in the plan body (HTML comment with erk:metadata-block markers) must survive all edits. Never strip or overwrite HTML comment blocks that contain erk:metadata-block markers." 21→ score: 9 22→--- 23→ 24→# One-Shot Workflow 25→ 26→The one-shot workflow enables fully autonomous planning and implementation from a single CLI command. It dispatches a GitHub Actions workflow that creates a plan and implements it without human intervention. 27→ 28→## End-to-End Pipeline 29→ 30→```
... [202 lines omitted] ...
104→`src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/register_one_shot_plan.py` performs four independent operations that `erk pr submit` normally handles at submit time. Each operation is best-effort -- failures are logged but don't block others: 111→The command outputs JSON results for each operation with success/error details. 124→The CLI write is the primary source; CI registration fills gaps when the CLI couldn't complete (e.g., network failure during dispatch). 213→2. If `rejected: true`, it extracts the `reason` and logs a GitHub Actions warning

1→--- 2→title: Codespace Machine Types 3→read_when: 4→ - "creating or configuring codespaces" 5→ - "choosing a machine type for codespace setup" 6→ - "debugging codespace creation failures" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "using `gh codespace create` to create a codespace" 9→ warning: "The machines endpoint returns HTTP 500 for this repo. Use `POST /user/codespaces` REST API directly. See the workaround section below." 10→last_audited: "2026-02-17 00:00 PT" 11→audit_result: clean 12→--- 13→ 14→# Codespace Machine Types 15→ 16→## Why `POST /user/codespaces` Instead of `gh codespace create` 17→ 18→<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/codespace/setup_cmd.py, setup_codespace --> 19→ 20→GitHub's REST endpoint `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/codespaces/machines` returns HTTP 500 for certain repositories, including the erk repository. Since `gh codespace create` calls that endpoint for machine type validation before creating anything, the entire command fails. This is a server-side GitHub bug — the endpoint works for some repos but not others, with no documented pattern for which ones fail. 21→ 22→The workaround bypasses validation entirely: `erk codespace setup` posts directly to `POST /user/codespaces` with the machine type as a raw string parameter and the repository's database ID. This means invalid machine type names produce a creation-time error rather than a pre-validation error, but in practice the set of valid machine types rarely changes. 23→ 24→This is one instance of a broader pattern where `gh` CLI subcommands make implicit API calls that fail. See [GitHub CLI Limits](../architecture/github-cli-limits.md) for the general pattern and other affected commands. 25→ 26→## Machine Type Selection 27→ 28→Claude Code agent sessions are memory-intensive — smaller machine types cause OOM kills during long sessions with large context windows. The default is `premiumLinux` for this reason. 29→ 30→| Machine Name | CPUs | RAM | Storage | When to use |
... [20 lines omitted] ...
44→- [GitHub CLI Limits](../architecture/github-cli-limits.md) — Broader pattern of `gh` subcommand failures from implicit API calls
